instructions.instruction: Generate a Makefile using the Modern GNU Make header (SHELL := bash, .ONESHELL:, .SHELLFLAGS := -eu -o pipefail -c, .DELETE_ON_ERROR:, .SUFFIXES:, MAKEFLAGS += --warn-undefined-variables, MAKEFLAGS += --no-builtin-rules) unless the user explicitly requests POSIX-compatible output.
instructions.why_given: Modern header ensures safe shell execution, reproducible builds, and early error detection.

instructions.instruction: Declare all non-file targets in a single .PHONY line at the top of the Makefile. Never omit .PHONY for targets like clean, test, all, install, help.
instructions.relevant_when: Generating any target that does not produce a file artifact
instructions.why_given: Without .PHONY, a file named 'clean' or 'test' silently prevents the target from running.

instructions.instruction: Use ?= for user-overridable variables (CC, CFLAGS, PREFIX, DESTDIR) and := for project-computed variables (SOURCES, OBJECTS).
instructions.why_given: ?= allows callers to override without editing the file; := prevents repeated shell expansion.

instructions.instruction: Use $(MAKE) not bare make for recursive sub-directory calls so that -j, -n, and -k flags are inherited by sub-makes.
instructions.relevant_when: Generating any target that calls make recursively
instructions.why_given: Bare make does not forward parent jobserver or dry-run flags, causing inconsistent parallel and CI builds.

instructions.instruction: Add order-only prerequisites (| $(BUILDDIR)) for build directory creation in large projects instead of inline mkdir -p in every recipe.
instructions.relevant_when: Generating C/C++ or multi-output projects with a dedicated build directory
instructions.why_given: Order-only prerequisites prevent spurious rebuilds when directory timestamps change.

instructions.instruction: Add error handling (|| { echo ...; exit 1; }) and .NOTPARALLEL declarations for Docker build, push, and deploy targets.
instructions.relevant_when: Generating Makefiles that include Docker or deployment targets
instructions.why_given: Docker operations are stateful and race-prone; missing error handling silently passes failed pushes.

instructions.instruction: Never hardcode credentials, tokens, or registry passwords in the Makefile. Use environment variables or external secret files.
instructions.relevant_when: Any target that authenticates to a remote service (Docker registry, package registry, cloud provider)
instructions.why_given: Credentials in Makefiles are committed to version control and exposed in build logs.

instructions.instruction: Use tabs for recipe indentation, never spaces. The Makefile spec requires tab characters; spaces cause 'missing separator' errors.
instructions.why_given: Make parses recipe lines by the presence of a literal tab character.

instructions.instruction: Document targets with ## comments (double-hash) immediately above each target so the help target can extract them with grep or awk.
instructions.relevant_when: Adding a help target to the Makefile
instructions.why_given: Self-documenting Makefiles reduce onboarding friction and match GNU Coding Standards.
